seoTitle: How to Change the Beginning Balance in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to change the beginning balance of an account in QuickBooks Online so your starting figures match your actual bank or account records.
intro: An incorrect beginning balance can throw off every reconciliation that follows, so fixing it early is worth the effort. This guide walks through how to locate and correct the beginning balance for an account in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Identify the account
steps.body: Determine which account has an incorrect beginning balance that needs adjusting.

steps.heading: Find the opening balance entry
steps.body: Locate the original opening balance transaction tied to that account, often set when the account was first created.

steps.heading: Edit the balance amount
steps.body: Update the opening balance entry to reflect the correct starting figure.

steps.heading: Reconcile to confirm accuracy
steps.body: Run a reconciliation afterward to make sure the corrected balance now matches your actual bank or account statement.

faq.question: Why would my beginning balance be wrong?
faq.answer: This can happen from an incorrect entry when the account was first set up, or from transactions dated before your QuickBooks start date.

faq.question: Will changing the beginning balance affect past reconciliations?
faq.answer: Yes, adjusting the beginning balance can affect previously completed reconciliations, so review them afterward.

faq.question: Can I change the beginning balance for any account type?
faq.answer: Yes, bank, credit card, and other balance sheet accounts can all have their beginning balance adjusted if needed.

faq.question: Should I consult an accountant before changing this?
faq.answer: For accounts with a longer transaction history, it's a good idea to have an accountant review the change to avoid unintended effects on your reports.
